site.name: Mandum Rimba
site.description: A map of deforestation, palm oil & mining concessions, and flood & landslide events, compiled from credible satellite and public data, for Indonesia's forests and the wildlife that calls them home.

about.title: About Mandum Rimba
about.intro: Mandum Rimba is an independent, non-profit observatory that distills credible satellite and public data on deforestation, palm oil and mining expansion, disasters, and threatened wildlife in Indonesia into one open map anyone can check.
about.nameBody: ‘Mandum’ means ‘all / everyone’ in Acehnese; ‘Rimba’ is the wild forest. Mandum Rimba means the forest that belongs to all of us, not to one company, one government, or one generation, but a shared home for the people and wildlife who live in it. The name is born in Aceh, where our data first begins.
about.whyBody: Every time forest turns into plantations, mines, or cleared land, elephants, tigers, and orangutans lose their home, and conflict with humans almost always ends badly for them. Many people don't know what is actually happening around where they live. Mandum Rimba assembles scattered data so anyone can see, understand, and act.
about.whatBody: We gather data from credible sources and show it as it is:
about.layer2: Palm, pulpwood, logging concessions, and the mining footprint
about.layer3: Protected & moratorium areas, National Parks, Wildlife Reserves, Nature Reserves, and more
about.p1Body: We gather and show scattered data, and let the reader judge for themselves. No accusations, no verdicts against anyone.
about.p2Body: Anything on the map traces back to its source dataset, with the retrieval date and license.
about.p3Body: The whole data pipeline and code are open source. Anyone can re-run it and get the same numbers.
about.p4Body: We don't sell data, run ads, or take sides.
about.openBody: Mandum Rimba is AGPL-3.0 licensed and open to contributions from everyone. Each dataset remains the property of its source, see the Data Sources and Credits pages.

home.heroTitle: When the forest disappears, they lose their home.
home.heroBody: Sumatran elephants and tigers, Bornean orangutans, Sulawesi anoa, Papuan birds-of-paradise, one by one they lose the space to live as forest turns into palm plantations, mines, and cleared land. From the land to its seas, we distill credible satellite and public data into one map anyone can understand, so everyone can see what is happening, grasp the consequences, and act.
home.threadsTitle: It began with a small conversation
home.threadsBody: Mandum Rimba grew out of small conversations on Threads, from people who care about the same thing. Here is part of the story.
home.principle1Body: We show official data and satellite imagery side by side, so you can read and compare them yourself.
home.principle2Body: Anything on screen traces back to its source dataset in at most two clicks.
home.principle3Body: Our pipeline is open: anyone can re-run it and get the same numbers.
home.mission1Body: These forests are the last home of the Sumatran elephant, the tiger, and the orangutan. Every hectare lost pushes them out and conflict with humans almost always ends badly for them.
home.mission2Body: Bare hills upstream mean floods and landslides downstream. We link disaster records to forest loss within the same watershed, so the connection is plain to see.
home.mission3Title: For those who work the land
home.mission3Body: Many people depend on this land for a living, and we are not here to judge. We show the data as it is, so everyone understands the consequences of what happens on the ground.
home.ctaBody: Awareness begins with clear information. Open the map, explore your region, and share what you find.
home.nameMandum: 'all / everyone' in Acehnese, the land where this watch begins
home.nameRimba: the wild forest, home to our wildlife
home.nameMeaning: Mandum Rimba means the forest that belongs to all of us, not to one company, one government, or one generation, but a shared home for the people and wildlife who live in it. The name is born in Aceh, where our data first begins.
home.publicInterest: Mandum Rimba is an independent, non-profit public-interest observatory. We don't sell data, run ads, or take sides. We simply assemble credible satellite and public data into one open map anyone can check, all of the code is open source, and every number traces back to its source.
home.campaignCaption: Get to know who lives near you. Threatened wildlife may be closer than you think.
home.ktpCtaSub: Make your forest ID and share it
home.supportCtaSub: Follow the journey and lend support

campaign.title: Who Lives Near You
campaign.intro: Protected wildlife often feels far away, in the forest, out at sea, in the news, in the numbers. Yet some of it might live not far from your home. We can't love what we don't know, so let's get to know the wildlife living near you first. Then introduce it to the people closest to you.
campaign.privacyLoc: Your location is only used to calculate distance, we don't store it.
campaign.privacyPhoto: Your photo is processed on your device and never stored. You can skip it too.
campaign.cropHint: Drag the photo to reposition, then adjust the zoom.
campaign.shareTagline: get to know who lives near you · mandumrimba.org
campaign.shareHint: On mobile, Share goes straight to WhatsApp/Instagram/Threads/X/Facebook. On desktop, download the image and post it to your story.
campaign.previewEmpty: Pick your location to build the card.

ktp.title: Forest Resident Card
ktp.intro: Resident cards trending on Threads? Let's make the jungle edition. Pick your city and we'll issue a 'national ID' for the wild neighbour nearest you, with its conservation status and habitat. We cannot love what we do not know.
ktp.validValue: AS LONG AS ITS FOREST STANDS
ktp.caption: A forest resident around {place} · {km}
ktp.photoHint: Your photo is processed on your device and never stored. You can skip it too.

map.title: Map
map.searchPlace: Search a place or paste coordinates…

viewHints.terrain: 3D globe with real elevation — tilt to see mountains and valleys

map.realmTourPlayHint: Fly the globe through all three realms

sundaland.name: Sundaland
sundaland.desc: Sumatra, Borneo and Java — home to tigers, orangutans and elephants.

wallacea.name: Wallacea
wallacea.desc: Sulawesi, Maluku and the Lesser Sundas — a singular transition zone: anoa, babirusa, Komodo dragon.

papua.name: Papua
papua.desc: New Guinea and its islands — birds-of-paradise, tree kangaroos and echidnas.

map.rotateHint: Tap to turn, hold to spin
map.popupDisclaimer: Public third-party data, shown as-is and possibly outdated. Names and boundaries come from the source dataset, not from us, and are not an allegation that any law was broken.
map.popupConcessionNote: Concession and mined-area boundaries come from public datasets for a given point in time and are shown for information only. The presence of a concession does not indicate any legal violation or particular activity. Tree cover loss inside or around a concession does not always mean deforestation and needs further, context-specific analysis.
map.lossNotDeforestation: Source: Hansen/UMD (Global Forest Watch), shown for information. Tree cover loss is not the same as deforestation and does not indicate any particular party or activity; interpretation needs further, context-specific analysis.

wetlandInfo.mangrove: Mangrove forest: a coastal vegetation belt that buffers erosion and storms, stores 'blue' carbon many times that of inland forest, and is a fish nursery and home for proboscis monkeys, crocodiles, and water birds. 2020 extent from satellite imagery (Global Mangrove Watch v3).
wetlandInfo.peatland: Peatland: carbon-rich wetland soil built up over millennia. Drained and burned it releases enormous carbon and drives the haze; kept wet it is vital habitat for orangutans, tigers, and swamp wildlife.

map.speciesHint: Type a plant's scientific name to see where it lives (e.g. Shorea, Nepenthes, Dipterocarpus).
map.rangeDerivedNote: Each dot is a real GBIF occurrence record (not an interpolated range).
map.speciesNone: No species of conservation concern recorded at this point.
map.docRange: Documented range (IUCN / KLHK), not a field observation
map.docFootnote: * documented range (IUCN / KLHK), not from observation records
map.photoCredit: 📷 Photos from Wikimedia Commons (CC / public domain), shown where a freely-licensed image exists.
map.sensitiveNote: Location coarsened to protect this collection-sensitive species.

region.notFound: Region not found, or the API could not be reached.
region.sourceNote: Source: Global Forest Watch (UMD/Hansen), refreshed daily. See methodology.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.intro: Every layer in Mandum Rimba has a source, a retrieval date, and a methodology. This page explains where the data comes from, how it is processed, and its limitations.
methodology.limitsBody: Satellite alerts have false positives; pixel resolution is 10-30 m; public concession boundaries can be outdated; watershed aggregation approximates by intersecting administrative regions. The methodology version is stamped on every derived record and every change is noted in this changelog.
methodology.disclaimerBody: Mandum Rimba assembles public third-party data and presents it as-is; it draws no conclusions, accuses no one, and does not assert that any party has broken the law. Names of companies, concessions, and places come from the source datasets, not from us. Overlaps between layers are context, not verdicts, and tree cover loss is not always illegal deforestation. We honour the presumption of innocence, and we welcome correction requests, which we review and fix.

data.title: Data
data.intro: CSV/GeoJSON downloads for datasets whose licenses permit redistribution. Other datasets link out to their original source.

sources.title: Data Sources
sources.intro: Every dataset shown on the map is listed here so you can verify it yourself: source organization, link, license, last update, coverage, and description. We never fabricate coordinates, habitat ranges, mining locations, or conservation data.
sources.gapsIntro: We could not find credible open data for the following. We name the provider it should come from rather than guess.
sources.disclaimerBody: Everything here is gathered from credible third-party public sources and shown as-is, unaltered. Company, concession, and location names come directly from the source datasets, not from us, and are not accusations. Mandum Rimba draws no legal conclusions and accuses no one: an overlap between layers (for example, tree cover loss inside a concession) is not a claim that any law was broken, and tree cover loss is not always illegal deforestation. We respect the presumption of innocence. If any party believes a data point is inaccurate, please contact us and we will review and correct it.

status.title: Pipeline status
status.intro: When each data source was last refreshed, a public trust signal. Jobs run automatically every day (WIB).
status.unavailable: Status unavailable, the API could not be reached.

credits.title: Credits & Acknowledgements
credits.intro: Mandum Rimba stands on the work of many: the people who build it, the organizations that open their data, and the open-source tools that run it. Thank you.
credits.openContribution: Mandum Rimba is open source (AGPL-3.0). Contributions are open to everyone,
credits.supportersIntro: A big thank you to the Rakan Rimba, the friends who chip in through Trakteer or PayPal. Your support is what keeps this going. Names are only shown with your permission.
credits.supportersQris: Many friends also gave quietly through QRIS, and I don't always know who to thank. Thank you, truly. If that's you and you'd like your name here, just DM me on Threads.
credits.supportersEmpty: None yet. Be the first to join this journey.
credits.dataTitle: With thanks to the data providers
credits.dataIntro: This data is opened freely by the organizations below. Full detail, licenses, and verification links are on the
credits.toolsIntro: Built with the following open-source tools and basemaps.
credits.licenseIntro: The code is open; the brand is the maintainer's. Each layer is licensed separately:
credits.licenseCodeDesc: Run, study, and improve it; if you deploy a modified version as a network service, you must share your source.
credits.licenseBrandDesc: Trademarks of the maintainer, not covered by the code license; forks must rebrand and keep a credit to mandumrimba.org.
credits.licenseDataDesc: belongs to each source under its own license, see the
credits.licenseGovernanceDesc: A single-steward model; contributions are under the Contributor License Agreement.

project.title: The project
project.intro: Mandum Rimba is built in the open, as a non-profit, public-interest project. This page explains where its source code stands and how you can get involved.
project.sourceBody: All of Mandum Rimba's code is open-source (AGPL-3.0). The intent is simple: anyone can inspect how it works, copy it, and help improve it.
project.sourceStatus: The repository is currently being prepared for public release, together with contribution guidelines so anyone can take part comfortably. The repository link will appear here once it's ready.
project.involveIntro: You don't need to write code to help. What we need most right now:

involveItems.title: Data corrections
involveItems.body: if a coordinate, status, or source is wrong, tell us with a link to the original source.

involveItems.title: Layer or species suggestions
involveItems.body: credible public data that deserves a place on the map.

involveItems.title: Design & methodology feedback
involveItems.body: what's confusing, or what could be made clearer.

involveItems.title: Spread the word
involveItems.body: share the map with people who care about Indonesia's forests and wildlife.

involveItems.title: Code contributions
involveItems.body: open as soon as the public repository and its contribution guide are ready.

project.contactBody: Have a correction, a suggestion, or a question? There's no dedicated inbox yet, so DM me directly on Threads at

support.title: Support the Rimba Universe
support.intro: Mandum Rimba has a sibling: Lam Rimba, a 3D conservation game where you step into the Indonesian rimba, rescue wildlife, nurse them back to health, return them to the wild, then heal the habitat. Both share one world, The Rimba Universe, and one simple belief: we cannot love what we do not know.
support.freeBody: It is built with Godot and, in its first phase, runs right on your phone, both iOS and Android. Free, so it can reach as many people as possible.
support.whyTitle: Why I opened the door to support
support.whyBody: Mandum Rimba, the map you are looking at, needs a server, a domain, and the data pipeline that keeps it alive and up to date every day. Lam Rimba, the game, is a bigger world still: full of 3D assets, sound, animation, and many things that take both time and money. For the first time I am opening the door to support, so both can keep growing.

useItems.title: Assets and production
useItems.body: 3D models of the wildlife and its forest, animation, music, and sound, down to the small details that make the rimba feel alive. Some I build myself, some I have to buy or make with paid tools.

useItems.title: Releasing to the app stores
useItems.body: the Apple App Store and Google Play developer accounts each carry their own registration fees. This is what lets Lam Rimba reach your phone, free to download.

useItems.title: Running and upkeep
useItems.body: the servers, domains, and data pipeline that keep Mandum Rimba and Lam Rimba running and updated, day after day.

useItems.title: Time to build it
useItems.body: most of my time and focus now goes here, tending this small rimba bit by bit until it is truly done.

support.freeHelpBody: Support does not have to be money. These matter just as much:
freeHelpItems: Tell others about Lam Rimba and Mandum Rimba.
Repost anything related to The Rimba Universe.
Invite a friend to get to know the rimba.
support.noteBody: Truly, please do not feel obliged. That you stopped by, read this far, and care at all is already more than enough. Whatever shape your support takes, I will never measure it by its size.
support.ctaBody: The most helpful way to support is via Trakteer (for Indonesia) or PayPal (international). Anything, however small, means a lot.
support.trakteerNote: Trakteer accepts QRIS, GoPay, OVO, DANA, ShopeePay, and cards, for supporters in Indonesia.
support.qrisBody: Your support helps Lam Rimba grow, and the game will always be free for everyone.
support.qrisDmNote: Transferred? Send your receipt via DM on Threads so I can add your name to the Rakan Rimba list. Only if you'd like to, of course.
support.qrisAnon: Many friends have given quietly through QRIS, and I don't always know who to thank. Thank you, truly. If that's you and you'd like your name on the list, just DM me on Threads.
support.transparencyNote: For transparency, I update the Rakan Rimba list periodically.
support.donorsBody: A big thank you to everyone who chips in through Trakteer or PayPal. However small, your support is what keeps this going. Names are only shown with your permission, please DM me on thread.
support.donorsEmpty: None yet. Be the first to join this journey.

footer.license: Open source (AGPL-3.0). Data belongs to its respective sources, see methodology.
footer.disclaimer: Presented descriptively from public data; not an accusation against any party.
footer.rights: All rights reserved under their respective licenses.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer & Terms
disclaimer.updated: Applies to your use of mandumrimba.org.
disclaimer.intro: Mandum Rimba is an independent, non-profit, public-interest observatory made for education and awareness. It compiles credible third-party public data about Indonesia's forests and shows it as-is. Please read the following before relying on anything here.
disclaimer.s1Body: This is a public-interest and educational project, not a legal, commercial, or investigative-journalism service. We publish data so the public can see and understand it. We do not sell it, run ads, or take sides.
disclaimer.s2Body: Every layer comes from a credible public source, cited on the Data Sources and Methodology pages, and is shown without alteration. Third-party data can be incomplete, out of date, or contain errors. It is provided on an 'as is' basis, with no warranty of accuracy or fitness for any purpose.
disclaimer.s3Title: Not an accusation, presumption of innocence
disclaimer.s3Body: Mandum Rimba draws no conclusions and makes no allegations against any person or company. Names of companies, concessions, and places come directly from the source datasets, not from us. An overlap between layers (for example, tree cover loss inside a concession) is context, not a verdict, and tree cover loss is not always illegal deforestation. Nothing here is a determination that any party has broken the law; we uphold the presumption of innocence.
disclaimer.s4Body: If you are an affected party, or you spot data you believe is inaccurate, misleading, or objectionable, please tell us. Send a message via Threads DM and we will review it in good faith and correct, annotate, or remove the item promptly. The right of reply is always open.
disclaimer.s5Body: Use this site at your own discretion. Mandum Rimba and its contributors are not liable for any loss or decision made in reliance on the information shown. For legal, commercial, or field decisions, verify against the original sources.
disclaimer.s6Body: The code is open source under AGPL-3.0. Each dataset remains the property of its source and is used under that source's licence; see the Data Sources and Credits pages.
disclaimer.s7Body: These terms may be updated as the project evolves. Continued use means you accept the current version.
disclaimer.contactNote: Corrections and right-of-reply requests are welcome and reviewed in good faith.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.updated: How Mandum Rimba handles your data.
privacy.intro: Mandum Rimba is built to need as little of your data as possible. There are no accounts, and we don't sell data. Here is exactly what happens.
privacy.s1Body: The 'Who lives near you' feature uses your location only inside your browser, to calculate distance, and never sends or stores it. Any photo you add for a Forest Resident Card is processed entirely on your device and is never uploaded. You can skip both.
privacy.s2Body: Anonymous, aggregate usage analytics (Google Analytics and Vercel Web Analytics) to understand which pages are useful, and a small functional cookie that remembers your light/dark theme. We do not build a personal profile of you.
privacy.s3Body: Pages load map tiles and assets from providers such as CARTO, Esri, Global Forest Watch, and public font/CDN hosts. As with any website, these may see your IP address as part of serving the request. Analytics is provided by Google and Vercel.
privacy.s4Body: We use a functional cookie only to remember your theme choice. Analytics may set its own cookies or identifiers; you can block these with your browser or an ad-blocker without breaking the site.
privacy.s5Body: Because we don't hold a personal profile of you, there is little to access or delete. If you have a privacy question or request, message us via Threads DM.
privacy.s6Body: We will update this page if our data practices change.
privacy.contactNote: Privacy questions and requests are welcome.

gate.title: Before you explore
gate.body: Mandum Rimba visualises public datasets from various organisations, for education and data exploration. We make no accusations, findings of fact, or legal conclusions about any individual, company, or institution.
gate.body2: Names, boundaries, and attributes come from their respective source datasets. An overlap between layers is visual context to aid analysis, not evidence or a conclusion. We uphold the presumption of innocence.
